summary: High‑energy set with scorching Shakedown>Samson and tight Sugar Magnolia
blurb: The show bursts out with a rare Shakedown Street → Samson & Delilah opener and rolls through a solid first set highlighted by Peggy-O and Big Railroad Blues. The second set stays on fire with seamless segues (Space → Not Fade Away, Eyes Of The World → Man Smart, Woman Smarter) and a ripping Sugar Magnolia. Overall the performance is strong despite a few miscues in Let It Grow.
review: On October 9, 1983 at Greensboro Coliseum the Dead delivered an electrifying concert that still feels fresh decades later. Kicking off with a blistering Shakedown Street into Samson & Delilah set the tone for a night of high‑octane playing; Jerry’s guitar was “scorching” on both the opener and the later Sugar Magnolia, while Bob turned his tremolo bar loose on Sugar Magnolia in what fans dubbed “Bob Gone Wild.” Brent’s keyboards and backing vocals lifted the transitions, especially during the Space → Not Fade Away flow, and the band kept the momentum rolling through Eyes Of The World into Man Smart, Woman Smarter. Though Let It Grow showed a few hiccups, the rest of the set—highlighted by Peggy‑O, Big Railroad Blues, and a tight Not Fade Away—was consistently strong, making this one of the more memorable shows of the fall ’83 tour.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings describe the performance as positive, praising scorching solos, tight segues and standout songs; two are mixed but contain no strong negative criticism. The consistent praise across multiple sources warrants a moderate positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Decent audience tape that captures the high‑energy performance and key transitions despite minor pitch/flatness issues, making it the most complete representation of the show.

key_highlights: Killer Shakedown Street → Samson & Delilah opener ignites the concert.
Bob Weir’s tremolo‑bar frenzy on Sugar Magnolia steals the spotlight.
Seamless segues (Space → Not Fade Away, Eyes Of The World → Man Smart, Woman Smarter) keep momentum high.

band_performance.Jerry: Scorching solos throughout—especially on Shakedown Street, Samson & Delilah and Sugar Magnolia—with only minor miscues at the end of Let It Grow and into Eyes Of The World.
band_performance.Bob: Tremolo‑bar wizardry on Sugar Magnolia (“Bob Gone Wild”) and strong playing on Big Railroad Blues; a botched Baby Blue is noted but not detrimental.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work and backup vocals shine, particularly during the Space → Not Fade Away segue, pushing the band’s energy forward.
